# Sports Performance Handbook

## Player Injury Prevention
Player injury prevention uses conditioning, monitoring, and workload management to reduce the likelihood of injury. Teams review training loads, recovery patterns, and movement quality to identify risk factors. Prevention programs are most effective when they combine medical oversight with coaching discipline.

## Training Load Monitoring
Training load monitoring tracks the stress imposed on athletes over time. Coaches and health staff review volume, intensity, and recovery to balance adaptation and fatigue. Good monitoring helps avoid overtraining and supports peak performance.

## Rehabilitation Planning
Rehabilitation planning coordinates exercise progression, medical supervision, and return-to-play criteria. A structured plan reduces the chance of re-injury and supports confidence. It also helps athletes resume performance at the right pace.
